licenses.issuingAgency: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development (DCCED)
licenses.applicationFee: $50 (biennial); $25 (for businesses with $5,000 or less in annual gross receipts)
licenses.renewalPeriod: Every two years (expires December 31 of even-numbered years)
requiredDocuments: Federal Employer Identification Number (FEIN) or Social Security Number
Business name and primary business address in Alaska
prerequisites: Must have a physical or registered address in Alaska
Corporations, LLCs, and partnerships must be registered with the Alaska Division of Corporations
Some professions require additional occupational licenses before applying for a general business license
licenses.processingTimeline: Online applications processed immediately; paper applications 5–10 business days
applicationProcess: Determine if your business requires any professional or occupational licenses in addition to the general business license
Register your business entity with the Alaska Division of Corporations, Business, and Professional Licensing at the DCCED website
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS at irs.gov
Apply online at the DCCED Online Licensing Portal or submit a paper application by mail
Pay the $50 biennial fee (or $25 if gross receipts are $5,000 or less)
Receive your Alaska Business License certificate and display it at your principal place of business
licenses.notes: An Alaska Business License is required for all businesses selling goods or services in Alaska. This is a state-level license; some municipalities such as Anchorage and Fairbanks may require additional local business licenses.

licenses.issuingAgency: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development (DCCED)
licenses.renewalPeriod: Every two years (expires December 31 of even-numbered years)
requiredDocuments: Federal EIN or Social Security Number
Proof of general liability insurance (required for commercial contracts)
Proof of workers' compensation insurance (required if you have employees)
prerequisites: Must register business entity with the Alaska Division of Corporations if operating as LLC, corporation, or partnership
Workers' compensation insurance is mandatory if you have one or more employees in Alaska
If performing janitorial work for government contracts, may need to comply with Alaska labor and wage regulations
applicationProcess: Register your business structure (LLC, sole proprietor, etc.) with the Alaska Division of Corporations
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS
Obtain general liability insurance and, if hiring employees, workers' compensation insurance
Apply for an Alaska Business License online through the DCCED portal
Pay the $50 biennial fee
Check with your local municipality (e.g., Municipality of Anchorage) for any additional local business license requirements
Renew your license before December 31 of each even-numbered year
licenses.notes: Alaska does not have a specific cleaning business license beyond the general Alaska Business License. However, if you perform any work involving hazardous materials or mold remediation, additional certifications may be required.

licenses.issuingAgency: Alaska Department of Environmental Conservation – Food Safety and Sanitation Program
licenses.applicationFee: $400 – $750 (state food service permit; varies by risk category and seating capacity)
requiredDocuments: Vehicle registration and proof of ownership
Proposed menu and food preparation procedures
Commissary agreement with an approved facility
HACCP plan (for certain high-risk food items)
Food manager certification (Alaska Food Worker Card or equivalent)
prerequisites: Must operate from an approved commissary for food storage, prep, and cleaning
Vehicle must pass a sanitation inspection by Alaska DEC
Food handler must have an Alaska Food Worker Card (available through the DEC)
Must comply with Municipality of Anchorage or local borough requirements if operating in those jurisdictions
licenses.processingTimeline: 3 – 6 weeks from application to permit issuance, including inspection scheduling
applicationProcess: Register your business with the Alaska Division of Corporations and obtain a state Business License
Obtain a Federal EIN from the IRS
Establish a commissary agreement with a licensed food establishment in Alaska
Ensure your food truck is equipped with proper handwashing sinks, a three-compartment sink, adequate refrigeration, and fire suppression equipment
Complete the Food Establishment Permit application and submit it to the Alaska DEC Food Safety and Sanitation Program with required documents
Schedule a pre-operational inspection with your regional DEC Environmental Health office
Pass the sanitation inspection and pay the permit fee
Obtain any required local permits from the municipality or borough where you will operate
Obtain a Food Worker Card from the Alaska DEC for all food handlers
Display all permits and certifications inside the food truck during operation
licenses.notes: Alaska's large geographic area means regulations can vary significantly between the Municipality of Anchorage, Fairbanks North Star Borough, and unorganized boroughs. Always check with local authorities before operating. Seasonal food trucks may apply for a temporary food service permit.
